use sqlx::PgPool;

async fn enrich_orders(pool: &PgPool, order_ids: &[i64]) -> Result<Vec<EnrichedOrder>, sqlx::Error> {
    // GOOD: batch queries with JOIN and IN clause
    let orders = sqlx::query_as::<_, OrderRow>(
        "SELECT id, customer_id, total FROM orders WHERE id = ANY($1)"
    )
    .bind(order_ids)
    .fetch_all(pool)
    .await?;

    let customer_ids: Vec<i64> = orders.iter().map(|o| o.customer_id).collect();
    let customers = sqlx::query_as::<_, CustomerRow>(
        "SELECT id, name, email FROM customers WHERE id = ANY($1)"
    )
    .bind(&customer_ids)
    .fetch_all(pool)
    .await?;

    let customer_map: std::collections::HashMap<i64, CustomerRow> =
        customers.into_iter().map(|c| (c.id, c)).collect();

    Ok(orders.into_iter().filter_map(|o| {
        customer_map.get(&o.customer_id).map(|c| EnrichedOrder {
            id: o.id,
            customer_name: c.name.clone(),
            total: o.total,
        })
    }).collect())
}

struct OrderRow { id: i64, customer_id: i64, total: f64 }
struct CustomerRow { id: i64, name: String, email: String }
struct EnrichedOrder { id: i64, customer_name: String, total: f64 }
